result.summary: Context7 exposes a public REST API described by OpenAPI, with some endpoints usable anonymously and higher-privilege operations requiring a Bearer API key; the site also documents OAuth endpoints but the corpus does not show a user-facing app-registration flow.

context7_api_key.label: Context7 API key
context7_api_key.setup: Open the [Context7 docs home](https://context7.com/docs) and use the **Get an API Key** link to mint an API key for higher rate limits and private repositories. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>` to the API.

surfaces.name: Context7 Public API
surfaces.notes: The OpenAPI schema shows mixed security: `GET /v2/libs/search` and `GET /v2/context` allow anonymous access (`security: [{}, { bearerAuth: [] }]`), while `GET /v2/libs/metrics`, `POST /v1/refresh`, and `GET /v2/policies` require `bearerAuth`. OAuth endpoints were machine-detected at `https://context7.com/api/oauth/authorize`, `https://context7.com/api/oauth/token`, and `https://context7.com/api/oauth/register`, but the corpus does not document a user-minted OAuth client acquisition flow, so no separate OAuth credential is registered.
